subject: English Language
question_text:
Change the following sentence into Passive Voice:
'The scientist discovered a new element.'

options.text: A new element was discovered by the scientist.
A new element is discovered by the scientist.
A new element has been discovered by the scientist.
The discovery of a new element was made by the scientist.

solution.final_answer: A new element was discovered by the scientist.

step_by_step: Identify Subject (Scientist), Verb (Discovered), Object (New Element).
For Simple Past: Object + was/were + V3 + by + Subject.
'A new element' + 'was' + 'discovered' + 'by the scientist'.

subject: English Language
question_text:
Convert the following into Indirect Speech:
She said, 'I will visit the museum tomorrow.'

options.text: She said that she will visit the museum tomorrow.
She said that she would visit the museum the next day.
She told that she would visit the museum tomorrow.
She says she would visit the museum the following day.

solution.final_answer: She said that she would visit the museum the next day.

step_by_step: Change 'will' to 'would' (tense shift).
Change 'tomorrow' to 'the next day' or 'the following day' (time reference shift).
Use 'that' connector.

subject: English Language
question_text: Passive Voice of: 'Who wrote this book?'

options.text: By whom was this book written?
Who was this book written by?
This book was written by whom?
Both A and B

step_by_step: For 'Who' questions: 'By whom' + auxiliary + object + V3 OR 'Who' + auxiliary + object + V3 + 'by'.
Both forms are grammatically correct in modern English.

subject: English Language
question_text: Indirect Speech of: The teacher said, 'The sun rises in the east.'

options.text: The teacher said that the sun rose in the east.
The teacher said that the sun rises in the east.
The teacher told that the sun rises in the east.
The teacher said the sun had risen in the east.

solution.final_answer: The teacher said that the sun rises in the east.

step_by_step: When the reported speech contains a universal truth or habitual fact, the tense remains unchanged.

subject: English Language
question_text: Passive Voice of: 'Please shut the door.'

options.text: The door should be shut.
You are requested to shut the door.
Let the door be shut.
Shut the door was requested by you.

solution.final_answer: You are requested to shut the door.

step_by_step: When 'Please' is used, the passive form usually starts with 'You are requested to'.

subject: English Language
question_text: Indirect Speech of: He said to me, 'Where are you going?'

options.text: He asked me where I was going.
He asked me where was I going.
He said to me where I was going.
He questioned me where am I going.

solution.final_answer: He asked me where I was going.

step_by_step: Change 'said to' to 'asked'.
The question form becomes an assertive form (Subject before Verb).
'Are you going' (Present Continuous) becomes 'I was going' (Past Continuous).

subject: English Language
question_text: Passive Voice of: 'They are playing cricket.'

options.text: Cricket is played by them.
Cricket was being played by them.
Cricket is being played by them.
Cricket has been played by them.

solution.final_answer: Cricket is being played by them.

step_by_step: For Present Continuous: Object + is/am/are + being + V3 + by + Subject.

subject: English Language
question_text: Indirect Speech of: My father said, 'Honesty is the best policy.'

options.text: My father said that honesty was the best policy.
My father said that honesty is the best policy.
My father told honesty is the best policy.
My father suggested honesty being the best policy.

solution.final_answer: My father said that honesty is the best policy.

step_by_step: Tense does not change for universal proverbs and established sayings.

subject: English Language
question_text: Passive Voice of: 'She has completed the assignment.'

options.text: The assignment was completed by her.
The assignment has been completed by her.
The assignment is completed by her.
The assignment had been completed by her.

solution.final_answer: The assignment has been completed by her.

step_by_step: For Present Perfect: Object + has/have + been + V3 + by + Subject.

options.text: Let the window be opened.
The window must be opened.
Open the window please.
Window should be open.

step_by_step: For commands/orders: Let + object + be + V3.

subject: English Language
question_text: Indirect Speech of: He said, "Alas! I am undone."

options.text: He said sadly that he was undone.
He exclaimed with sorrow that he was undone.
He exclaimed that he is undone.
He cried that he was undone.

solution.final_answer: He exclaimed with sorrow that he was undone.

step_by_step: "Alas" indicates sorrow. Change "said" to "exclaimed with sorrow".

subject: English Language
question_text: Passive Voice of: "Someone has stolen my pen."

options.text: My pen has been stolen by someone.
My pen has been stolen.
Someone has my pen stolen.
My pen was stolen.

step_by_step: When the subject is "someone", "people", etc., it is often omitted in passive voice for brevity and natural flow.

subject: English Language
question_text: Indirect Speech of: The boss said to the employee, "Don't be late tomorrow."

options.text: The boss told the employee not to be late the next day.
The boss ordered the employee to not be late tomorrow.
The boss warned the employee to be late the next day.
The boss said to the employee don't be late following day.

solution.final_answer: The boss told the employee not to be late the next day.

step_by_step: Change "Don't" to "not to". Change "tomorrow" to "the next day".

options.text: He is known by me.
He is known to me.
He was known to me.
He is known with me.

step_by_step: The verb "know" takes the preposition "to" instead of "by" in passive voice.

subject: English Language
question_text: Indirect Speech of: She said to me, "May you live long!"

options.text: She said that I might live long.
She wished that I might live long.
She prayed that I may live long.
She blessed me that you might live long.

solution.final_answer: She wished that I might live long.

step_by_step: Change "said to" to "wished/prayed". Change "may" to "might".
